DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF LUIS GALICIA'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES AND DOCUMENT PRODUCTION OF EMPORIUM MALL, LLC. TO DEMAND FOR PRODUCTION (SET NO. ONE) AND REQUEST OF MONETARY SANCTIONS. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF THE INCIDENT REPORT IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. THE WRITTEN AGREEMENT BETWEEN SECURITY CONTRACTOR AND PROFESSIONAL SECURITY CONSULTANTS ("PSC") AND EMPORIUM MALL PROVIDES THE "INCIDENT REPORT SHALL BE COMPLETED AND SUCH INCIDENT REPORTS SHALL REMAIN THE PROPERTY OF THE MANAGER". PLAINTIFF'S ARGUMENT AN INDEPENDENTLY RETAINED SECURITY CONTRACTOR'S REPORT TO ITS PRINCIPAL CANNOT PER SE BE A PROTECTED PRIVILEGED COMMUNICATION IS OVERRULED. DEFENDANT EMPORIUM MALL'S HAS SUSTAINED ITS BURDEN OF ESTABLISHING THE INCIDENT REPORT IS SUBJECT TO THE ATTORNEY-CLIENT PRIVILEGE. WITH PSC PRESENTLY A PARTY TO THE ACTION DISCOVERY MAY BE UNDERTAKEN TO OBTAIN FACTS TO SUPPORT THE ALLEGATION THE DOMINANT PURPOSE OF THE REPORT WAS NOT LITIGATION. BOTH PARTIES' REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PRO TEM: STEVEN STEIN, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

Off Calendar Dec-10-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Alese K. Lesser, esq. (310-706-3400) of Hahn Legal Group for Plaintiff/Moving party via Court Call.
Martin Gattas, esq. (213-689-8500) of Cruser, Mitchell, Novitz, Sanchez, Gaston & Zimet, LLP for Defendant Professional Security Consultants via Court Call.
Amber Lee Kelly, esq. (415-949-1900) of Law Offices of Demler, Armstrong & Rowland, LLP for Defendant, Emporium Mall, LLC.

ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF LUIS GALICIA's Motion To Compel Further Responses And Document Production Of Emporium Mall, LLC. To Demand for Production (Set No. One) And Request of Monetary Sanctions.

Plaintiff's motion to compel production of the incident report is denied without prejudice. The written agreement between security contractor and Professional Security Consultants ("PSC") and Emporium Mall provides the "Incident Report shall be completed and such incident reports shall remain the property of the manager". Plaintiff's argument an independently retained security contractor's report to its principal cannot per se be a protected privileged communication is overruled. Defendant Emporium Mall's has sustained its burden of establishing the incident report is subject to the attorney-client privilege. With PSC presently a party to the action discovery may be undertaken to obtain facts to support the allegation the dominant purpose of the report was not litigation. Both parties' request for sanctions is denied.

Order signed in open court.

Judge Pro Tem: Steven Stein, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Alese K. Lesser, Esq., Hahn Legal Group, Telephone 310-706-3400, appearing for Plaintiff; (CourtCall)
Amber Lee Kelly, Esq., Demler, Armstrong & Rowland, LLP., Telephone 415-949-1900, appearing for Defendant Westfield Group, LLC.;

Ruling - ARGUED. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Emporium Mall, LLC's motion to strike portions of the first amended complaint is granted in its entirety without leave to amend but without prejudice to plaintiff Luis Galicia filing a motion to amend if and when he ever obtains sufficient information to allege punitive damages liability against Emporium Mall. Mr. Galicia concedes that the language "For costs of suit and attorney fees" should be stricken from the first amended complaint. Mr. Galicia has not alleged sufficient ultimate facts to support punitive damages liability against Emporium Mall nor does it appear that he can do so in good faith at this time, so the punitive damages allegations and prayer are also stricken from the first amended complaint. Order is signed.
